mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment signals improving labor income and consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS direction over 20-day returns is below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window, or the sign flips negative during expansions.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the central bank is actively tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS crosses 4.5%, the 20-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 55% of instances over any 5-year window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in both industrial production (supply) and retail sales (demand) indicate broad-based economic strength with balanced growth, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If over any 2-year period, instances where both INDPRO and RSAFS rise produce a negative average 20-day SPX return with p-value < 0.05, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPIAUCSL > 200), rising payroll employment amplifies wage pressure, forcing the central bank to tighten further, which ultimately depresses equity prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If in months where CPIAUCSL > 200 and PAYEMS rises, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more than half the time over any 10-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, which directly supports GDP growth and leads to higher S&P 500 returns within the following week as investor sentiment improves.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward SPX returns is less than 0.05 over a 5-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0%, it signals a weakening labor market that will reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings over the following quarter, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If in months after UNRATE crosses 5.0%, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases over any 10-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high inflation and high Federal Funds rate create a stagflationary environment: elevated discount rates squeeze valuations while persistent inflation erodes real earnings, both dragging on S&P 500 returns over the quarter.
regime_hint: high inflation and tight monetary policy
failure_condition: If in months when both CPIAUCSL > 200 and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the 60-day forward SPX return averages positive over any 5-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE > 5.5%), rising retail sales signal unexpected consumer resilience, which revises growth expectations upward and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If in months when UNRATE > 5.5% and RSAFS rises, the 60-day forward SPX return is negative more than half the time over any 10-year window, reject.
